post-natal.definition: Happening after the birth of a baby.
post-natal.example: Post-natal depression is common among new parents.

depression.definition: A mental health condition causing deep sadness, hopelessness, and loss of interest.
depression.example: Depression can make even small tasks feel impossible.

acquisition.definition: The act of getting or receiving something new.
acquisition.example: The acquisition of parental responsibility can trigger depression.

parental.example: Parental responsibility brings both joy and challenges.

symptoms.definition: Signs that show you have a sickness or condition.
symptoms.example: Common symptoms include deep sadness and trouble sleeping.

irritability.definition: The state of being easily annoyed or made angry.
irritability.example: Irritability is a common symptom of post-natal depression.

despair.example: She felt deep sadness and despair after the birth.

insomnia.definition: Not being able to sleep even when you want to.
insomnia.example: Insomnia made her exhaustion even worse.

psychosis.definition: A very serious mental condition where a person loses touch with reality.
psychosis.example: In extreme cases, post-natal depression can lead to psychosis.

post-partum.definition: The period of time after giving birth (same as post-natal).
post-partum.example: Post-partum depression affects many new mothers each year.

psychological.definition: Related to the mind and how it works.
psychological.example: Depression is a psychological disorder that needs treatment.

disorder.definition: A medical condition where something in the body or mind is not working normally.
disorder.example: Post-natal depression is a serious psychological disorder.

terrified.example: She was terrified she would somehow hurt the baby.

nappy.definition: Soft cloth or paper worn by a baby to catch waste (British English for diaper).
nappy.example: She was afraid of hurting the baby during a nappy change.

ashamed.definition: Feeling shame or embarrassment about something.
ashamed.example: She was so ashamed of her feelings that she told no one.

surgery.definition: The place where a doctor sees patients (British English); also a medical operation.
surgery.example: She took the baby to the GP's surgery for a checkup.

breastfeed.definition: To feed a baby with milk from the mother's breast.
breastfeed.example: She could not breastfeed because her nipples hurt too much.

nipples.definition: The raised parts on the breast where milk comes out.
nipples.example: Her nipples hurt so much that feeding felt like pins going through her.

pins.definition: Thin, sharp pointed pieces of metal.
pins.example: The pain felt like pins going through her.

bottle-feeding.definition: Feeding a baby with milk from a bottle instead of the breast.
bottle-feeding.example: Bottle-feeding made her feel like even more of a failure.

failure.definition: A person or thing that does not succeed; feeling you are not good enough.
failure.example: She felt like a failure as a mother.

confess.definition: To admit something that is difficult or embarrassing to say.
confess.example: She had to confess that she wanted to die.

bleak.example: She had never felt so bleak in her entire life.

dense.definition: Thick and heavy; hard to see through.
dense.example: A dense cloud of misery had descended on her.

descended.definition: Came down upon; arrived and covered.
descended.example: A cloud of depression descended on her after the birth.

misery.example: She was trapped in a cloud of misery for months.

therapy.definition: Treatment for mental or emotional problems, usually by talking to a professional.
therapy.example: Her partner made her go to therapy.

matter-of-fact.definition: In a calm, practical way, without showing emotions.
matter-of-fact.example: She was planning to kill herself in a matter-of-fact way.

babyhood.definition: The time when someone was a baby.
babyhood.example: The therapist asked her to think about her own babyhood.

cousin.definition: The child of your aunt or uncle.
cousin.example: Her cousin told her what happened when she was a baby.

abroad.definition: In a foreign country; away from home country.
abroad.example: Her parents went abroad for a month without her.

alien.definition: Something or someone that feels very strange and unfamiliar.
alien.example: The baby felt like an alien to her, not like her own child.

indiscernibly.definition: So slowly or slightly that you almost cannot notice it.
indiscernibly.example: She began slowly, indiscernibly, to get better.

acceptance.definition: Agreeing that something is true and being at peace with it.
acceptance.example: She came to an acceptance that she was his mother.

narrative.definition: A story or account that explains events and makes sense of them.
narrative.example: Finding a narrative that makes sense of your feelings can help.

sufficient.definition: Enough; as much as is needed.
sufficient.example: Just knowing there is an explanation can be sufficient to help.

impulses.definition: Sudden strong feelings that make you want to do something without thinking.
impulses.example: Talking about your impulses can help you control them.

perceive.definition: To see or understand something in a particular way.
perceive.example: Talking helps us perceive the baby as a baby, not a threat.

projecting.definition: Putting your own feelings or thoughts onto someone else without realizing it.
projecting.example: Parents sometimes project their fears onto their baby.

monster.definition: A scary, imaginary creature; something that frightens you.
monster.example: The baby is not a monster, even when the crying feels unbearable.

ghost.definition: The spirit of a dead person; here, a painful memory from the past.
ghost.example: Old feelings can haunt us like a ghost from our past.

fantasize.definition: To imagine something you want to happen, especially something unlikely.
fantasize.example: It is normal to fantasize about escaping when you feel overwhelmed.

fantasy.definition: Something you imagine that is not real.
fantasy.example: A fantasy is not harmful when it stays just a thought.

minimize.definition: To make something smaller or less important.
minimize.example: Talking about feelings can help minimize their power over you.

non-judgemental.definition: Not making harsh decisions about whether someone is right or wrong.
non-judgemental.example: We all need someone non-judgemental to talk to.

unapologetically.definition: Without saying sorry or feeling ashamed.
unapologetically.example: You need someone you can be fully and unapologetically yourself with.

warrant.example: Do not think you are not in a bad enough state to warrant help.

hesitate.definition: To pause or wait before doing something because you are not sure.
hesitate.example: Do not hesitate to seek professional help.

exaggerating.definition: Making something seem bigger or stronger than it really is.
exaggerating.example: Hormones may be exaggerating your emotions after birth.

withdraw.definition: To pull back from people or activities; to become less involved.
withdraw.example: If your feelings cause you to withdraw from your baby, seek help.

interacting.definition: Communicating and spending time with someone.
interacting.example: Depression can make it hard to keep interacting with your family.

achiever.definition: A person who is successful and gets good results.
achiever.example: At work, she was a high achiever with high standards.

lacking.definition: Not having enough of something; falling short.
lacking.example: She compared herself to other mothers and found herself lacking.

grated.definition: Irritated or annoyed in a strong way.
grated.example: When the baby cried, it grated on her rather than making her want to help.

stressful.definition: Causing a lot of worry and pressure.
stressful.example: Leaving the house felt stressful with a new baby.

forceps.definition: A medical tool shaped like tongs, used to help deliver a baby.
forceps.example: She needed forceps during the delivery.

invasive.definition: Entering your body or personal space in an unwelcome way.
invasive.example: She found the whole birth procedure very invasive.

nightmarish.definition: Like a bad dream; very frightening and upsetting.
nightmarish.example: She kept reliving the nightmarish experience of the birth.

reliving.definition: Experiencing something again in your mind as if it is happening now.
reliving.example: She kept reliving the birth experience in her dreams.

dragged.definition: Forced yourself to go somewhere even though you did not want to.
dragged.example: She dragged herself to the mother-and-baby group.

pretend.definition: To act as if something is true when it is not.
pretend.example: She was ready to pretend she was fine again.

announced.example: One woman announced that she was not coping.

coping.example: She admitted that she was not coping with the new baby.

courage.definition: The ability to do something that is scary or difficult.
courage.example: She took her courage in both hands and spoke up.

collages.definition: Art made by sticking different materials like paper and cloth onto a surface.
collages.example: The group made collages by sticking bits of cloth on paper.

crafts.definition: Activities where you make things by hand.
crafts.example: The group did crafts together while they talked.

sticky.definition: Difficult or unpleasant (informal); also literally something that sticks.
sticky.example: Their sticky start did not seem to cause lasting damage.

hormonal.definition: Caused by or related to hormones in the body.
hormonal.example: She believed her depression was hormonal.

weird.definition: Strange; unusual in a way that makes you feel uncomfortable.
weird.example: However weird your feelings seem, do not keep them to yourself.

visualization.definition: A technique where you picture something in your mind to explore your feelings.
visualization.example: The exercise uses guided visualization to help you understand yourself.

scenario.definition: A situation that you imagine or describe.
scenario.example: Visualize a scenario in your mind's eye.

reception.definition: A room or area where visitors are received or welcomed.
reception.example: The first room is a reception room where you meet visitors.

vulnerability.definition: The state of being open to being hurt or affected emotionally.
vulnerability.example: The second room represents your difficulty and vulnerability.

regretful.definition: Feeling sorry about something you did or did not do.
regretful.example: In the room of difficulty, you may feel regretful or ashamed.

frustrated.definition: Feeling upset because you cannot do what you want or things are not working.
frustrated.example: She felt frustrated that she could not bond with her baby.

dissatisfied.definition: Not happy or pleased with something.
dissatisfied.example: She felt dissatisfied with herself as a mother.

shallowly.definition: Not deeply; taking only small, light breaths.
shallowly.example: If you were breathing shallowly, try to breathe normally again.

pride.definition: A feeling of happiness and satisfaction about something you did well.
pride.example: In the positive room, you feel a sense of pride as a parent.

ambiguous.definition: Having more than one meaning; not clear or certain.
ambiguous.example: Parenting brings up ambiguous feelings that are hard to explain.

flooded.definition: Completely filled with something, especially a strong emotion.
flooded.example: Someone who can hear you when you feel flooded with love.

owe.definition: To have a duty or responsibility to someone.
owe.example: You owe it to yourself and your child to seek help.

present.definition: Here, feeling mentally engaged and aware in the moment.
present.example: You deserve to feel present and correct in your life.

crèche.definition: A place where babies and young children are cared for while parents are nearby.
crèche.example: The support group had a crèche so mothers could talk freely.
